project.name: HpAgent
project.description: HpAgent is an intelligent conversational agent framework based on a hand-brain separation architecture, featuring Temporal Workflow orchestration, nsjail OS-level sandbox isolation, and Hindsight long-term memory. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: agents.yaml
nodes.summary: Defines three multi-agent mode personas (default, analyst, executor) with model selectors and system prompts for task-based routing by the Orchestrator.

nodes.name: development-sql.yaml
nodes.summary: Temporal Server dynamic configuration for development with PostgreSQL, enabling worker versioning, eager workflow start, and update workflow execution features.

nodes.name: models.yaml
nodes.summary: Central model API configuration defining provider credentials and fallback chains for chat, embedding, image, and reasoning model categories used across the HpAgent framework.

nodes.name: environment.yaml
nodes.summary: Runtime environment detection prompts that inject Docker or WSL container context into the system prompt, guiding the model about filesystem layout and shell syntax.

nodes.name: guidance.yaml
nodes.summary: Behavioral discipline and style guidance prompts controlling the model's tone, verbosity, tool use enforcement, and channel-specific output formatting rules.

nodes.name: identities.yaml
nodes.summary: Channel-specific agent identity declarations mapping NapCat, Console, Web, and default channels to distinct personas with tailored communication styles and behavioral rules.

nodes.name: system.yaml
nodes.summary: System-level prompt templates for cross-channel detection, context file headers, content truncation markers, prompt injection blocking, and memory recall instructions.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yaml
nodes.summary: Docker Compose orchestration defining eight services (hpagent, Redis, NapCat QQ bot, Temporal with PostgreSQL, Hindsight with pgvector) on a shared bridge network with health checks and volume persistence.
nodes.languageNotes: Multi-service orchestration with explicit depends_on conditions, health checks, named volumes, and a shared bridge network for cross-service DNS resolution.

nodes.name: hpagent
nodes.summary: Main HpAgent application service built from ./src, exposing port 8082 with Temporal, Hindsight, and Redis dependencies, running in privileged mode for nsjail namespace creation.

nodes.name: redis
nodes.summary: Redis 7 Alpine in-memory session store with health check, exposing port 6379 for session caching and pub/sub messaging.

nodes.name: napcat
nodes.summary: NapCat QQ bot service using mlikiowa/napcat-docker, exposing port 6099 with persistent QQ login credentials and cache volumes.

nodes.name: temporal-postgres
nodes.summary: PostgreSQL 16 Alpine database for Temporal Server, storing workflow event history and metadata with health check via pg_isready.

nodes.name: temporal
nodes.summary: Temporal Server 1.26.2 with PostgreSQL backend, exposing port 7233 for workflow orchestration with auto-setup and health check.

nodes.name: temporal-web
nodes.summary: Temporal Web UI 2.34.0 providing a browser-based interface for workflow execution history, status monitoring, and debugging on port 8088.

nodes.name: hindsight-postgres
nodes.summary: PostgreSQL 16 with pgvector extension for Hindsight memory service, storing vector embeddings and structured memory events.

nodes.name: hindsight
nodes.summary: Hindsight 0.6.1 long-term memory service with local BGE-M3 embeddings and BGE reranker, external pgvector database, and extended 600s startup wait for model loading.
nodes.languageNotes: Extended startup period (1200s) accommodates first-run model downloads for BGE-M3 and reranker; local embedding provider eliminates cloud API dependency for vector generation.

nodes.name: mistake.md
nodes.summary: Troubleshooting guide documenting three infrastructure issues: docker-compose v1 incompatibility with Docker Engine 29.x, Hindsight embedded pg0 data corruption loop, and hardcoded 300s startup timeout in the Hindsight container image.

nodes.name: 问题诊断与修复建议.md
nodes.summary: Deleted Chinese-language diagnostic and repair guide for the v8 system. Content is no longer available in the working tree.

nodes.name: hindsight-memory-best-practices.md
nodes.summary: Deleted best practices guide for Hindsight memory integration. Content is no longer available in the working tree.

nodes.name: napcat-api-reference.md
nodes.summary: Deleted API reference document for NapCat QQ bot integration. Content is no longer available in the working tree.

nodes.name: architecture-and-data-flow.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive v4 architecture analysis documenting the four-layer design (Orchestrator, Harness, Session, Sandbox), data format definitions, end-to-end sequence diagrams, and Temporal Workflow data flow with session/ID generation logic.

nodes.name: cross-client-memory-design.md
nodes.summary: Design analysis and migration plan for cross-client memory sharing, detailing six root-cause issues preventing QQ-Web memory sharing and presenting a six-step refactoring solution with AccountService, ChannelRouter, and PostgreSQL integration.

nodes.name: napcat.md
nodes.summary: Full OneBot v11 event type reference and implementation guide for the NapCat channel normalize_message method, covering message, notice, request, and meta-event handling with sender_id and metadata extraction logic.

nodes.name: temporal.md
nodes.summary: Technical decision report for introducing Temporal Workflow orchestration, covering architecture analysis, Workflow/Activity mapping, two-phase migration plan, risk assessment, and detailed implementation code for AgentWorkflow and Activities.

nodes.name: architecture-and-data-flow.md
nodes.summary: v5 architecture documentation describing the five-layer design with the new Account layer and ChannelRouter, updated end-to-end sequence diagrams, data format conversion chains, and cross-channel event mixing in the long-running Workflow.

nodes.name: cross-client-memory.md
nodes.summary: v5 cross-client memory implementation detailing AccountService, ChannelRouter, long-running Workflow signal mechanism, PostgreSQL database schema, account binding flows, and docker-compose service topology for unified multi-channel memory.

nodes.name: durable.md
nodes.summary: Design reference for the durable storage infrastructure layer, defining three storage protocols (KeyValueStore, FileStore, PubSub) with asyncpg/SQLAlchemy PostgreSQL implementation, AioFileStore atomic file operations, Redis cache/pub-sub, and an 8-table Agent memory schema with pgvector support.

nodes.name: storage-layer.md
nodes.summary: v5 storage layer implementation summary covering the file listing, protocol definitions, error handling with StoreError, backend implementations (AioFileStore, SqlKeyValueStore, Redis), dependency injection container, and comparison with the legacy session repository.

nodes.name: 01-refactoring-plan.md
nodes.summary: Sandbox module refactoring plan replacing in-process tool execution with nsjail OS-level sandbox isolation, defining NsjailConfig, NsjailExecutor, runner.py in-jail script, and Redis result persistence across four implementation phases.

nodes.name: 02-evaluation.md
nodes.summary: Security, performance, compatibility, and operations evaluation of the nsjail sandbox refactoring, concluding that the 20-60ms latency overhead is acceptable for a 23/25 security score improvement.

nodes.name: 03-summary.md
nodes.summary: Implementation summary for the nsjail sandbox refactoring with 22/22 tests passing, documenting file changes (3 added, 5 modified), architecture before/after comparison, design decisions, configuration reference, and rollback plan.

nodes.name: 04-workspace-evaluation.md
nodes.summary: Evaluation of the multi-user workspace directory scheme with per-user session isolation, SQLite metadata database, nsjail bind mount integration, session lifecycle management, and risk mitigation analysis.

nodes.name: 05-workspace-summary.md
nodes.summary: Implementation summary for the multi-user workspace directory system with 45/45 tests passing, documenting the directory skeleton, SQLite database schema, nsjail bind mount integration, session lifecycle activities, and 23 workspace-specific test cases.

nodes.name: 06-hindsight-evaluation.md
nodes.summary: Evaluation of Hindsight long-term memory system integration, analyzing architecture impact, recall/retain/reflect data flows, security isolation, migration strategy with five implementation phases, graceful degradation design, and risk assessment.

nodes.name: 07-hindsight-summary.md
nodes.summary: Implementation summary for Hindsight memory system integration with 65/65 tests passing, documenting the HindsightClient API wrapper, three Temporal Activities, context builder memory injection, Workflow orchestration changes, and graceful degradation matrix.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview documentation describing the hand-brain separation architecture, five-layer structure, Docker service stack, key design decisions, and memory module design with Hindsight integration for the HpAgent intelligent dialogue agent framework.

nodes.name: 08-architecture.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive v6 architecture document covering all five layers (Channels, Orchestration, Harness, Sandbox, Workspace) with module dependency diagrams, startup sequence, message processing flow, agentic loop details, Docker deployment topology, data model dictionary, configuration reference, and test coverage.

nodes.name: 多Agent架构最终迭代指导文档.md
nodes.filePath: docs/version/v7/多Agent架构最终迭代指导文档.md
nodes.summary: Final iteration of multi-agent architecture design document after five rounds of adversarial review, detailing the ControlStrategy pattern, Orchestrator pure scheduling engine, BaseAgent interface, and production-ready contract specifications for four orchestration modes.

nodes.name: 多agent架构选型迭代.md
nodes.summary: Complete record of all five rounds of multi-agent architecture design and adversarial review, including initial proposal, three rounds of critique-and-revise cycles, and final validated interface contracts for Supervisor, Workflow, Handoff, and Council orchestration modes.

nodes.name: 项目流程时序图.md
nodes.summary: Mermaid sequence diagrams illustrating the full startup flow, single-agent and multi-agent message processing pipelines, component dependency graph, and recommended code reading order for the HpAgent project.

nodes.name: Phase1到Phase2过渡计划.md
nodes.summary: Phase 1 to Phase 2 transition plan documenting the completed core agent components (types, interfaces, orchestrator, strategies, bus, registry, compensation), the Phase 2 deliverables including Supervisor and Council strategies, OrchestratorAsAgent recursive composition, and step-by-step implementation plan.

nodes.name: Phase2到Phase3实施总结.md
nodes.summary: Phase 2 to Phase 3 implementation summary covering the completion of three ControlStrategy implementations, recursion composition via OrchestratorAsAgent, the RealLLM abstractions (Planner, Reviewer, Judge), dead code cleanup, and Architecture overview after Phase 3 completion.

nodes.name: 问题诊断与修复建议.md
nodes.summary: Diagnostic report identifying 11 runtime issues from production log analysis, including Hindsight API timeout causing 70s latency, duplicate recall calls, missing circuit breaker, workspace creation idempotency bugs, and prioritized fix recommendations with file-level code references.

nodes.name: hindsight-memory-best-practices.md
nodes.summary: Hindsight memory integration best practices guide covering NapCat-to-Hindsight field mapping, document ID and segmentation strategies, tag system design, retain and recall optimization, timestamp standardization, privacy compliance, and prioritized implementation checklist.

nodes.name: napcat-api-reference.md
nodes.summary: NapCat API reference manual extracting message models, sender information, 23 message segment types, core APIs (send/get message, group info, user info), event types, and key insights for HpAgent memory module integration.

nodes.name: tools-layer.md
nodes.summary: Tools layer (Hands Layer) architecture document detailing the BaseTool abstraction, ToolRegistry, ToolFactory, DynamicTool, full execution chain from LLM tool_call through nsjail isolation to runner.py, with security model and key design decisions.

nodes.name: 001_accounts.sql
nodes.summary: SQL migration defining the core account and session schema with four tables (accounts, account_bindings, sessions, events) using PostgreSQL with foreign key relationships, cascading deletes, JSONB columns, and composite indexes.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses ON DELETE CASCADE for referential integrity across account bindings and sessions, JSONB for flexible event content and metadata storage.

nodes.name: accounts
nodes.summary: Core accounts table with account_id as TEXT PRIMARY KEY and created_at timestamp.

nodes.name: account_bindings
nodes.summary: Cross-channel account binding table mapping channel_type and channel_user_id to a unified account_id with cascading delete.

nodes.name: sessions
nodes.summary: Sessions table tracking active state per account with channel type and timestamps.

nodes.name: events
nodes.summary: Events table storing ordered events within each session with JSONB content and metadata, enforcing unique event index per session.

nodes.name: clear-hindsight.sh
nodes.summary: Shell utility to clear all stored Hindsight memories via REST API first, falling back to direct PostgreSQL truncation when API is unavailable, with dry-run and force-db options.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses dual-path cleanup strategy: API-first with HTTP status code handling, then direct database truncation via docker compose exec for resilient fallback.

nodes.name: show_status
nodes.summary: Displays current cleanup configuration including bank ID, API endpoint, and operation mode.

nodes.name: clear_via_api
nodes.summary: Attempts to delete the Hindsight bank via REST API DELETE request with HTTP status code handling.

nodes.name: clear_via_postgres
nodes.summary: Directly truncates all PostgreSQL user tables in a single transaction with CASCADE to handle foreign key dependencies.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Main entry point coordinating status display, user confirmation, API-first cleanup with PostgreSQL fallback.

nodes.name: dev-reset.sh
nodes.summary: Development utility script for killing the running HpAgent process and cleaning up orphaned Temporal workflows from the task queue to enable clean debug restarts.

nodes.name: kill_hpagent
nodes.summary: Finds and kills running HpAgent main.py processes with SIGTERM followed by SIGKILL if needed.

nodes.name: clean_temporal
nodes.summary: Queries Temporal for all running workflows on the task queue and terminates each one with a descriptive reason.

nodes.name: start-all.sh
nodes.summary: Hindsight Docker entrypoint script with embedded pg0 data integrity check, configurable dependency waiting (database and LLM), graceful shutdown handler with SIGTERM forwarding to child processes, and service startup orchestration for API and Control Plane.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements a robust graceful shutdown pattern using bash trap with SIGTERM forwarding to child PIDs and configurable timeout for PostgreSQL WAL flush.

nodes.name: cleanup
nodes.summary: Graceful shutdown handler forwarding SIGTERM to child processes with configurable timeout and SIGKILL fallback.

nodes.name: migrate-memory-format.py
nodes.summary: Hindsight memory format migration script that detects old-format memories (per-message document_id, missing timestamps, role-only context) and migrates them to new best-practice format with session-level document_id, ISO 8601 timestamps, and channel-aware context.

nodes.name: detect_format
nodes.summary: Detects whether a single memory item uses old, new, or mixed format based on document_id prefix, timestamp presence, and context field pattern.

nodes.name: migrate_item
nodes.summary: Transforms a single memory item from old format to new format including document_id, timestamp, context, and tag migration.

nodes.name: _cleanup_hindsight_memories
nodes.summary: Async function that deletes specified document_ids from Hindsight via REST API with HTTP status tracking.

nodes.name: _collect_old_document_ids
nodes.summary: Scans backup records and collects all old-format document_ids for cleanup targeting.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: CLI entry point orchestrating format detection, statistics reporting, migration file writing, and optional Hindsight API cleanup.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package docstring providing a comprehensive architecture overview of the HpAgent project, describing the five-layer architecture (orchestration, harness, resources, sandbox, session, storage, memory, workspace, account, common) and key design decisions including hand-brain separation, Temporal Workflow, and multi-channel account unification.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Account package init exporting Account data model and AccountService for cross-channel identity resolution.

nodes.name: account_service.py
nodes.summary: AccountService implementing channel-type and sender-id to unified account_id resolution with JSON file persistence and in-memory binding index for reverse lookups.

nodes.name: AccountService
nodes.summary: Maps channel identity pairs (channel_type, channel_user_id) to unified UUID account_ids with JSON-backed persistence and dual indexing.

nodes.name: models.py
nodes.summary: Account dataclass model representing a unified user account with multi-channel binding support, serialization to/from dictionary for JSON persistence.

nodes.name: Account
nodes.summary: Unified user account dataclass with account_id UUID, multi-channel binding dictionary, timestamps, and JSON serialization methods.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Multi-agent package init re-exporting the complete public API including types, interfaces, orchestration strategies, LLM abstractions, infrastructure components, factory functions, and agent implementations.
nodes.languageNotes: Python package barrel file re-exporting 45 public symbols across 12 submodules.

nodes.name: adapters.py
nodes.summary: ReActAgent adapter wrapping HarnessRunner as a BaseAgent implementation to bridge the single-agent harness into the multi-agent orchestration architecture.

nodes.name: ReActAgent
nodes.summary: Adapts HarnessRunner.process_turn() to the BaseAgent.execute() interface, enabling the single-agent runner to participate in multi-agent orchestration.

nodes.name: bus.py
nodes.summary: InMemoryMessageBus implementing the pure communication channel for agent-to-agent and orchestrator-to-agent messaging using asyncio.Queue with capability-based routing.

nodes.name: InMemoryMessageBus
nodes.summary: Process-internal message bus using asyncio.Queue for capability-based message routing, supporting send, broadcast with response collection, and listen with async iteration.

nodes.name: compensation.py
nodes.summary: Compensation framework with per-instance CompensationRegistry to avoid global singleton conflicts in recursive orchestration, and CompensationHandler abstract base class for rollback logic.

nodes.name: CompensationHandler
nodes.summary: Abstract base class for rollback handlers that compensate completed tasks on failure.

nodes.name: CompensationRegistry
nodes.summary: Per-instance registry mapping task_type strings to CompensationHandler instances, avoiding global singleton conflicts in recursive orchestration composition.

nodes.name: composite.py
nodes.summary: OrchestratorAsAgent enabling recursive composition by wrapping any Orchestrator as a BaseAgent, supporting nested orchestration patterns like Supervisor embedding Workflow.

nodes.name: OrchestratorAsAgent
nodes.summary: Wraps any Orchestrator instance as a BaseAgent for recursive composition, checking sub-task failures and delegating result aggregation to a ResultAggregator.

nodes.name: context.py
nodes.summary: Layered execution context with concurrent-safe SharedMemory (CAS-based), SessionState for conversation history, RuntimeConfig for timeout and retry settings, and ExecutionContext as the unified container.

nodes.name: SharedMemory
nodes.summary: Concurrent-safe key-value store for agent-shared state with namespace isolation and atomic compare-and-set operations.

nodes.name: factory.py
nodes.summary: Orchestrator factory providing ResourcePoolAdapter to bridge ResourcePool into CallLLM protocol, and one-line builder functions build_supervisor, build_council, and build_workflow for constructing pre-configured Orchestrator instances.

nodes.name: ResourcePoolAdapter
nodes.summary: Adapts ResourcePool.generate() to the CallLLM callable protocol for use with LLMPlanner, LLMReviewer, and LLMJudge implementations.

nodes.name: build_supervisor
nodes.summary: Builds a Supervisor-mode Orchestrator with RealLLMPlanner and RealLLMReviewer for dynamic LLM-driven task decomposition and review.

nodes.name: build_council
nodes.summary: Builds a Council-mode Orchestrator with parallel agent voting and LLM or majority-based judging.

nodes.name: build_workflow
nodes.summary: Builds a Workflow-mode Orchestrator from a static DAG of tasks and dependencies.

nodes.name: interfaces.py
nodes.summary: Defines core multi-agent abstractions — BaseAgent, ControlStrategy, AgentRegistry, and MessageBus interfaces for the agent orchestration layer.

nodes.name: BaseAgent
nodes.summary: Core BaseAgent interface defining the baseagent contract for multi-agent orchestration.

nodes.name: ControlStrategy
nodes.summary: Core ControlStrategy interface defining the controlstrategy contract for multi-agent orchestration.

nodes.name: AgentRegistry
nodes.summary: Core AgentRegistry abstract defining the agentregistry contract for multi-agent orchestration.

nodes.name: MessageBus
nodes.summary: Core MessageBus interface defining the messagebus contract for multi-agent orchestration.

nodes.name: llm_agent.py
nodes.summary: LLMAgent wraps a model client with message history formatting and tool-calling loop, implementing the BaseAgent interface for LLM-driven task execution.

nodes.name: LLMAgent
nodes.summary: LLMAgent orchestrates LLM inference with message building, history formatting, and tool-calling loop as a BaseAgent implementation.

nodes.name: orchestrator.py
nodes.summary: Orchestrator implements the multi-agent execution engine: plan-based task scheduling with batch execution, lifecycle management, and compensation handling.

nodes.name: Orchestrator
nodes.summary: Orchestrator drives plan-based multi-agent execution with batch scheduling, agent lifecycle management, and Saga-style compensation rollback.

nodes.name: registry.py
nodes.summary: InMemoryAgentRegistry provides thread-safe agent registration and capability-aware agent lookup for the orchestration layer.

nodes.name: InMemoryAgentRegistry
nodes.summary: InMemoryAgentRegistry manages agent registration, health tracking, and capability-based best-match selection.

nodes.name: runner.py
nodes.summary: MultiAgentExecutor configures and runs multi-agent workflows by building an orchestrator with supervisor or council strategies and synthesizing results via LLM.

nodes.name: MultiAgentExecutor
nodes.summary: MultiAgentExecutor sets up orchestrator with strategy, executes tasks, and synthesizes outputs via LLM-based result aggregation.

nodes.name: strategies.py
nodes.summary: Implements all agent orchestration strategies: WorkflowControlStrategy (DAG-based), RealLLMPlanner/Reviewer/Judge (LLM-driven planning), SupervisorControlStrategy, and CouncilControlStrategy.

nodes.name: ConditionEvaluator
nodes.summary: Evaluates task predicates and branch conditions to drive conditional workflow execution.

nodes.name: ResultAggregator
nodes.summary: Aggregates multi-agent task results into a single consolidated output.

nodes.name: WorkflowControlStrategy
nodes.summary: DAG-based workflow strategy managing task dependencies, batch scheduling, and retry logic.

nodes.name: LLMPlanner
nodes.summary: Abstract interface for LLM-based task planning from high-level goals.

nodes.name: LLMReviewer
nodes.summary: Abstract interface for LLM-based review of completed task outputs.

nodes.name: LLMJudge
nodes.summary: Abstract interface for LLM-based best-result selection from multiple candidates.

nodes.name: StubLLMPlanner
nodes.summary: Stub implementation of LLMPlanner returning a single default task for testing.

nodes.name: StubLLMReviewer
nodes.summary: Stub implementation of LLMReviewer returning no additional tasks for testing.

nodes.name: MajorityJudge
nodes.summary: Vote-based judge selecting the most common result from multiple candidates.

nodes.name: RealLLMPlanner
nodes.summary: LLM-driven planner using tool calls or JSON responses to decompose goals into task DAGs.

nodes.name: RealLLMReviewer
nodes.summary: LLM-driven reviewer that analyzes completed tasks and may propose follow-up tasks.

nodes.name: RealLLMJudge
nodes.summary: LLM-driven judge that selects the best result from multiple candidates with majority fallback.

nodes.name: SupervisorControlStrategy
nodes.summary: Supervisor pattern strategy with LLM-driven planning, review, and iterative refinement.

nodes.name: CouncilControlStrategy
nodes.summary: Council pattern strategy coordinating multiple agents with parallel execution and LLM judging.

nodes.summary: Core type definitions for the multi-agent system including Task, TaskResult, ExecutionPlan, BatchOutcome, and supporting enums/structs.

nodes.name: TaskStatus
nodes.summary: Data class representing taskstatus with status/metadata fields.

nodes.summary: Data class representing agenthealth with status/metadata fields.

nodes.summary: Data class representing errorinfo with status/metadata fields.

nodes.name: ExecutionMetrics
nodes.summary: Data class representing executionmetrics with status/metadata fields.

nodes.summary: Core model defining the task structure for agent orchestration.

nodes.summary: Multi-stage Docker build for the HpAgent service: Python 3.11-slim base with nsjail sandbox installation and entrypoint.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the harness module, re-exporting HarnessRunner and Temporal activity functions.

nodes.name: activities.py
nodes.summary: Temporal workflow activities for the agentic loop: inject, process_turn, archive_session, reflect, and metrics_report.

nodes.name: inject
nodes.summary: Injects an external event into a session for processing.

nodes.name: process_turn_activity
nodes.summary: Processes a single turn in the agentic loop as a Temporal activity.

nodes.name: archive_session_activity
nodes.summary: Archives a completed session's data to long-term storage.

nodes.summary: Periodic reflection activity that triggers memory consolidation.

nodes.summary: Batch reflection processing for multiple sessions.

nodes.name: metrics_report_activity
nodes.summary: Periodic metrics collection and reporting activity.

nodes.summary: Application entry point: initializes dependencies, configures logging, and starts the HpAgent worker with asyncio event loop.

nodes.name: __init__.py
nodes.summary: Package barrel for the memory module, exporting HindsightClient and related types.

nodes.name: hindsight_client.py
nodes.summary: HindsightClient provides long-term memory storage and retrieval via the Hindsight vector memory service, with BGE-M3 embeddings and pgvector similarity search.

layers.name: Orchestration Layer
layers.description: Temporal Workflow orchestration for the agentic loop, HarnessRunner brain coordinator, and application entry points that bootstrap the hand-brain separation architecture

layers.name: Multi-Agent Layer
layers.description: Multi-agent collaboration system with Supervisor, Council, and Workflow orchestration strategies, LLM-driven planning, agent registry, message bus, and compensation-based error handling

layers.name: Sandbox Layer
layers.description: nsjail OS-level process isolation for secure tool execution, multi-channel communication adapters (NapCat QQ, Console, Web), and pluggable tool registry for the hands layer

layers.name: Session Layer
layers.description: Conversation session context management, event stream modeling, and session persistence with Hindsight long-term memory integration for multi-turn interaction history

layers.name: Resource Layer
layers.description: LLM model client pool with unified Anthropic and OpenAI-compatible API interface, provider health checking, automatic failover, rate limiting, and credential encryption

layers.name: Memory Layer
layers.description: Hindsight long-term vector memory client for BGE-M3 embeddings, pgvector similarity search, and conversation recall/retain/reflect operations

layers.name: Data Layer
layers.description: Storage abstraction protocols with Redis/File/Postgres backends, workspace metadata SQLite database, account identity unification, SQL migrations, and shared common types, interfaces, and error hierarchies

layers.name: Infrastructure Layer
layers.description: Docker Compose multi-service orchestration, Dockerfile build configuration, Temporal and Hindsight service topologies, application YAML configuration, LLM prompt templates, and operational maintenance scripts

layers.name: Documentation Layer
layers.description: Architecture design documents, sequence diagrams, API references, migration plans, evaluation reports, and troubleshooting guides spanning v4 through v9 architecture iterations

layers.name: Test Layer
layers.description: Unit, integration, and end-to-end tests for multi-agent orchestration strategies, sandbox isolation, Hindsight memory operations, workspace lifecycle, and a WebSocket test client for service connectivity

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: The README.md introduces HpAgent as an intelligent conversational agent framework based on the hand-brain separation architecture. It describes the five-layer design, the Docker service stack, key design decisions around Temporal Workflow orchestration and nsjail sandbox isolation, and the Hindsight long-term memory integration. This document provides the conceptual roadmap for understanding every component in the tour ahead.

tour.title: Application Entry Point and Deployment
tour.description: main.py is the application entry point that initializes all dependencies, configures JSON-structured logging, and starts the asyncio event loop that drives the HpAgent worker. The Dockerfile builds the container image using a multi-stage Python 3.11-slim base with nsjail installed for sandbox isolation, while entrypoint.sh provides the minimal shell bootstrap that execs into the Python application at container startup.
tour.languageLesson: Multi-stage Docker builds use multiple FROM statements: the builder stage installs dev dependencies and compiles assets, while the final stage copies only runtime artifacts into a minimal base image, keeping the production image small and secure.

tour.title: Configuration Foundation
tour.description: The configuration model in orchestration/config.py defines all runtime settings using Pydantic-style dataclasses, covering providers, model connections, Temporal and Redis endpoints, sandbox isolation parameters, workspace paths, Hindsight memory service URLs, and multi-agent settings. The models.yaml centralizes provider credentials and fallback chains for chat, embedding, image, and reasoning model categories, acting as the single source of truth for LLM API configuration across the entire framework.

tour.title: Shared Foundation Types and Interfaces
tour.description: The common module defines the vocabulary shared across all layers: types.py contains unified data types including Event, UnifiedMessage, ToolCall, ToolResult, and ModelResponse with all enum definitions; interfaces.py defines the four core abstraction contracts using Python Protocol and ABC classes (IResources, ISandbox, IChannel, ITool); and errors.py establishes the typed exception hierarchy with an ErrorCode enum covering agent, session, sandbox, tool, and model error domains. These files are the most imported across the codebase because every other module depends on these shared definitions.
tour.languageLesson: Python Protocol classes define structural subtyping — any object with the required methods satisfies the protocol without explicit inheritance. This enables loose coupling between layers: the sandbox layer can implement ISandbox without importing the protocol definition at runtime.

tour.title: Temporal Workflow Engine
tour.description: The orchestration module wraps Temporal Workflow, the deterministic execution engine that guarantees the agentic loop survives process crashes and machine failures. workflow.py defines three Temporal workflows: OrchestrationWorkflow for agentic session processing, ReflectWorkflow for memory consolidation, and MetricsReportWorkflow for periodic reporting. worker.py manages the Temporal Worker lifecycle — registration of workflows and activities with the namespace and task queue, then starting the polling loop that waits for workflow tasks to execute.
tour.languageLesson: Temporal Workflow code must be deterministic — no random numbers, no system time, no direct network calls. All side effects (LLM calls, file I/O, database operations) must be wrapped in Activities, which are the only components allowed to perform nondeterministic operations.

tour.title: The Brain: Harness Agentic Loop
tour.description: HarnessRunner in runner.py orchestrates the core agentic loop: context building from project files and system prompts, LLM generation via the resource pool, tool execution through the sandbox, and multi-turn conversation management. The activities.py file registers five Temporal Activities (inject, process_turn, archive_session, reflect, and metrics_report) that form the building blocks of the workflow. Building on the Temporal engine from Step 5, this is where the brain's reasoning actually happens — the harness coordinates the model's thinking with tool execution in a deterministic loop.

tour.title: Multi-Agent Orchestration Layer
tour.description: The multi-agent subsystem extends the single-agent harness into a collaborative orchestration framework. interfaces.py defines the core abstractions (BaseAgent, ControlStrategy, AgentRegistry, MessageBus), orchestrator.py implements plan-based task scheduling with compensation rollback, and types.py provides the data types (Task, ExecutionPlan) that flow through every orchestration operation. These types are among the most depended-on modules in the codebase according to the fan-in analysis.
tour.languageLesson: The compensation (saga) pattern in orchestrator.py ensures that if a batch of agent tasks partially fails, previously completed tasks are rolled back via registered CompensationHandler instances. This is critical for maintaining consistency when multiple agents collaborate on a shared goal.

tour.title: Agent Strategies and Execution Context
tour.description: strategies.py implements four orchestration modes: DAG-based WorkflowControlStrategy, hierarchical SupervisorControlStrategy, parallel CouncilControlStrategy, and the RealLLMPlanner/Reviewer/Judge trio for LLM-driven planning with adversarial review. context.py provides the layered execution context with CAS-based SharedMemory for concurrent safety and SessionState for conversation history, while bus.py implements InMemoryMessageBus for capability-based agent-to-agent routing using asyncio.Queue.
tour.languageLesson: The Context and SharedMemory modules use compare-and-swap (CAS) semantics to provide safe concurrent access across multiple agents operating within the same orchestration scope. This avoids locks while ensuring that only one agent can modify shared state at a time.

tour.title: The Hands: Sandbox Isolation
tour.description: nsjail.py configures OS-level process isolation with configurable CPU, memory, and filesystem constraints for secure tool execution. runner.py is the in-jail script that receives commands via argv and returns JSON results, while sandbox.py implements the ISandbox interface with a ToolRegistry for tool registration and per-session workspace directory management.
tour.languageLesson: nsjail provides operating system-level sandboxing by using Linux namespaces (mount, PID, network, user) and cgroups to isolate processes. The sandbox bind-mounts only the specific workspace directory into the jail, preventing the agent from accessing any other files on the host system.

tour.title: LLM Resource Pool
tour.description: model_client.py provides a unified inference interface supporting both Anthropic and OpenAI-compatible APIs with streaming responses and tool-calling support. resource_pool.py maintains a pool of provider connections with health checking, rate limiting, and automatic failover, ensuring the system remains available even when individual providers fail.
tour.languageLesson: The ModelClient abstracts over different LLM providers by normalizing the request format (messages, tools, system prompt) and response format (content, tool_calls, stop_reason). Each provider adapter translates between this normalized format and the provider-specific API, enabling transparent provider switching without changing calling code.

tour.title: Session Persistence and Long-Term Memory
tour.description: session/models.py defines the SessionContext and SessionEvent dataclasses for multi-turn conversation tracking. session/store.py persists session state with JSON serialization and integrates with Hindsight for memory recall, while hindsight_client.py wraps the Hindsight vector memory API for long-term storage using BGE-M3 embeddings and pgvector similarity search.
tour.languageLesson: Hindsight operates on three core operations: retain (store a memory document with vector embeddings), recall (retrieve semantically similar memories via cosine similarity on pgvector), and reflect (ask the LLM to synthesize patterns across multiple recalled memories). The BGE-M3 embedding model generates multilingual embeddings that capture semantic meaning regardless of the input language.

tour.title: Infrastructure and Database Schema
tour.description: The docker-compose.yaml defines the full multi-service production topology with eight services: the HpAgent application, Redis for session caching, NapCat QQ bot for one channel adapter, Temporal Server with PostgreSQL for workflow orchestration with a Web UI for monitoring, and Hindsight with a separate pgvector PostgreSQL for vector memory storage. The migration file 001_accounts.sql defines the core database schema with four tables (accounts, account_bindings, sessions, events) that implement cross-channel account unification using foreign key relationships, JSONB columns for flexible event metadata, and composite indexes for query performance. Together these represent the full operational surface of the HpAgent system.
tour.languageLesson: Docker Compose service dependency ordering with depends_on ensures containers start in the correct sequence (Hindsight waits for pgvector PostgreSQL, Temporal waits for its PostgreSQL, HpAgent waits for both Temporal and Hindsight). Named volumes persist database state across container restarts, while health checks ensure the orchestrator does not route traffic to services that are not ready.
